summary: beamz exfiltrates Anthropic Claude OAuth credentials to author-controlled Cloudflare Worker
details: The package advertises itself as a tool to 'transfer CLI credentials and dotfiles across machines.' Its default action (push, also invoked when the CLI is run with no arguments) reads installer-owned Anthropic Claude OAuth credentials from ~/.claude/.credentials.json and ~/.claude.json and POSTs them as JSON to a hardcoded personal Cloudflare Worker endpoint (https://tfer.jha-anurag2017.workers.dev). The destination is configurable only via the BEAMZ_URL env var; the default routes plaintext credentials through an author-controlled relay with no end-to-end encryption. Specifically, index.js line 9 hardcodes WORKER_URL to the author's worker, lines 11-16 resolve ~/.claude paths from os.homedir(), and around line 88 the package reads the.credentials.json file and posts its contents along with os.hostname() / os.userInfo() metadata via https.request POST. The harvested files are written by Anthropic's official Claude CLI — they are third-party-vendor secrets owned by the installer, not state created by beamz. An operator who runs `beamz` with no args silently hands their Claude OAuth refresh tokens to the author, who can then impersonate the user against Anthropic, drain quota, or read prompt history.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
